law: Some iPad apps, such as games, require a full-screen experience.
These apps don’t participate in iPad multitasking, and they don’t support resizable windows when run on a Mac.
To ensure these behaviors, the apps include the key with a value of in their file.

This sample shows how to configure an iPad app that requires full-screen mode on iPad to take advantage of the native resolution of the Mac display for a pixel-perfect, edge-to-edge, full-screen experience.
An app that requires full screen on iPad — that is, an app that includes the key with a value of in its file — doesn’t take advantage of the entire display when run on a Mac in full-screen mode.
Many apps that require full-screen mode when running on iPad look equally as good on Mac when appearing in a window.
You can use the app to explore Touch Alternatives on your Mac with Apple silicon.
Use a mouse or trackpad to drag the circle around the window.
Press and hold Option to use a trackpad as a virtual touch screen.
The array should include only those values that make sense for the app.
With earlier versions of Xcode, developers can use the Property List template.
